titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Berkeley College, Yale University, Paul Mellon Fellowship Correspondence
Berkeley College, Yale University, Paul Mellon Fellowship Correspondence
titlestmt.author: compiled by Daniel Hartwig

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday January 10, 2025 at 06:21

did.unittitle: Berkeley College, Yale University, Paul Mellon Fellowship correspondence
did.abstract: The material consists of correspondence concerning the Paul Mellon Fellowship maintained by the master of Berkeley College, Yale University.

odd.head: General note
odd.p: Forms part of Yale Record Group 13-C-2 (YRG 13-C-2), Records of the master of Berkeley College.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The materials were transferred from the master of Berkeley College, Yale University, 2008.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to the materials is restricted. See Collection Contents for details.
Some records in this finding aid have been redacted, as they include student names, donor names, and other restricted data. These records will not appear in the published finding aid.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Copyright for materials authored or otherwise produced as official business of Yale University is retained by Yale University. Copyright status for other collection materials is unknown. Transmission or reproduction of materials protected by U.S. Copyright Law (Title 17, U.S.C.) beyond that allowed by fair use requires the written permission of the copyright owners. Works not in the public domain cannot be commercially exploited without permission of the copyright owners. Responsibility for any use rests exclusively with the user.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Berkeley College, Yale University, Paul Mellon Fellowship Correspondence (RU 1085). Manuscripts and Archives, Yale University Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Yale University records are arranged and described at the accession level by the creating office. The University Archives creates collection level descriptive records, but typically does no further arrangement and description at the accession level.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: The Paul Mellon Fellowship is offered to an unmarried member of the Senior Class in Yale College for study at Clare College, Cambridge, for the academic year.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The material consists of correspondence concerning the Paul Mellon Fellowship maintained by the master of Berkeley College, Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: The materials are arranged by correspondent.
